burial.plot_notes: Highland Cemetery Great Falls Montana per FA — buried with husband

narrative_blocks.header: Treasurer of the medical auxiliary; died at ninety-three
narrative_blocks.body: Joanne Mary McGinnis Northrop spent decades as treasurer of the Women's Medical Auxiliary for both Cascade County and the Montana State Medical Association, building an institutional life alongside her husband's medical practice in Great Falls while also traveling with him to California and Hawaii for golf and maintaining a condo in Whitefish, Montana for skiing and lake summers. She died in Eden Prairie, Minnesota on December 2, 2023 at age ninety-three — one of the most recently deceased members of the Hooker-line family tree, and among the most long-lived, her Montana and Minnesota years carrying a genealogical thread forward from a nineteenth-century New York City family into the twenty-first century.

narrative_blocks.body: Joanne Mary McGinnis Northrop spent decades as treasurer of the Women's Medical Auxiliary for both Cascade County and the Montana State Medical Association, building an institutional life alongside her husband's medical practice in Great Falls while also traveling with him to California and Hawaii for golf and maintaining a condo in Whitefish, Montana for skiing and lake summers. She died in Eden Prairie, Minnesota on December 2, 2023 at age ninety-three — one of the most recently deceased members of the Hooker-line family tree, and among the most long-lived, her Montana and Minnesota years carrying a genealogical thread forward from a nineteenth-century New York City family into the twenty-first century.

burial.plot_notes: Highland Cemetery Great Falls Montana per FA

narrative_blocks.header: Great Falls physician; his wife lived to ninety-three
narrative_blocks.body: Doctor Arthur Kimball Northrop practiced medicine in Great Falls, Montana, where his wife Joanne devoted her years as treasurer for the Women's Medical Auxiliary for both Cascade County and the Montana State Association — an intimate domestic partnership between a physician's work and its institutional support, sustained through decades of Montana winters, golf trips to California and Hawaii, and summers at their Whitefish condo. Joanne outlived him by twenty-eight years, dying in Eden Prairie, Minnesota in December 2023 at age ninety-three — one of the most recently deceased members of the Hooker family tree, carrying a genealogical line from Hartford 1636 to Minnesota 2023.
